methods.name: Trial & Error
methods.description: Systematically try different solutions and learn from failures. The most fundamental problem-solving method in nature and science.

methods.name: Brainstorming
methods.description: Generate a large quantity of ideas in a group setting without immediate criticism or judgment.

methods.name: Analogical Reasoning
methods.description: Transfer knowledge from a source domain to a target domain by identifying structural similarities.

methods.name: Inversion
methods.description: Flip the problem statement or assumed direction of causality to reveal new solutions.
trigger_conditions: Stuck in one direction of thinking
examples: Instead of 'how to catch mice', 'how to get mice to leave'

methods.name: Extreme Scenario
methods.description: Push parameters to their extreme values to understand system behavior and find breaking points.

methods.name: 40 Inventive Principles (TRIZ)
methods.description: Systematically apply 40 proven patterns of invention derived from analysis of millions of patents. Each principle represents a category of solutions that have repeatedly solved problems across domains.

methods.name: 76 Standard Solutions (TRIZ)
methods.description: Apply 76 standard solutions organized into 5 classes for solving substance-field (Su-Field) model problems.
trigger_conditions: System can be modeled as substances and fields
examples: Adding a field to complete a Su-Field model

methods.name: Morphological Box
methods.description: Decompose a system into independent parameters and enumerate all possible combinations to find novel configurations.

methods.name: Six Thinking Hats
methods.description: Parallel thinking method: deliberately switch between six modes (facts, emotions, caution, optimism, creativity, process) to examine a problem from all angles.

methods.name: Design Thinking
methods.description: Human-centered innovation process: empathize, define, ideate, prototype, test. Iterate rapidly with user feedback.

methods.name: First Principles Thinking
methods.description: Break down a problem to its most fundamental truths and rebuild from there, ignoring assumptions and analogies.
trigger_conditions: Current solutions all make the same tradeoff
examples: SpaceX reusable rockets (from raw material cost, not rocket price)

methods.name: 5 Whys
methods.description: Iteratively ask 'why' to drill down from surface symptoms to root cause.

methods.name: Gradient Descent
methods.description: Iteratively move in the direction of steepest improvement. Applicable metaphorically to any optimization problem: take small steps, measure, adjust direction.

methods.name: Ensemble Learning
methods.description: Combine multiple weak models to create a strong one. Diversity of approaches reduces overall error through voting, averaging, or stacking.

methods.name: Transfer Learning
methods.description: Apply knowledge learned in one domain to a different but related domain, dramatically reducing the need for new data.
examples: Pre-trained vision models for medical imaging

methods.name: Adversarial Generation (GAN)
methods.description: Pit two systems against each other (generator vs discriminator) to drive continuous improvement through competition.

methods.name: Attention Mechanism
methods.description: Dynamically focus computational resources on the most relevant parts of the input, ignoring irrelevant information.

methods.name: Evolutionary Algorithm
methods.description: Mimic natural selection: generate population of solutions, select best, mutate and recombine, repeat. Escapes local optima through population diversity.

methods.name: Ant Colony Optimization
methods.description: Use emergent swarm intelligence: simple agents following local rules with pheromone-like positive feedback find globally optimal paths.

methods.name: Immune System Strategy
methods.description: Learn to detect and neutralize novel threats by maintaining a diverse repertoire of detectors that adapts over time.

methods.name: Entropy Maximization
methods.description: Systems naturally evolve toward maximum entropy (disorder). Harness or counteract this tendency: either work with entropy or actively pump against it.

methods.name: Quantum Superposition
methods.description: Maintain multiple states simultaneously until measurement collapses the system. Metaphorically: explore multiple solutions in parallel before committing.

methods.name: Principle of Least Action
methods.description: Nature always chooses the path that minimizes action. Design systems to follow paths of least resistance rather than forcing unnatural behavior.
examples: Default settings that guide toward best practice

methods.name: Catalysis
methods.description: Introduce a substance that dramatically accelerates a reaction without being consumed. Metaphorically: find the small intervention that unlocks large-scale change.

methods.name: Self-Assembly
methods.description: Design components so they spontaneously organize into desired structures through local interactions, without centralized control.

methods.name: Topological Transformation
methods.description: Change the shape or connectivity of a problem while preserving its essential structure. A donut and coffee cup are topologically identical.
trigger_conditions: Need to reframe the problem space

methods.name: Fixed Point Theorem
methods.description: Under certain conditions, a function will always have a point that maps to itself. Find the equilibrium where forces balance rather than fighting them.

methods.name: Information Entropy
methods.description: Quantify uncertainty and information content. Maximize information where it's valuable, minimize where it's noise.

methods.name: Incentive Design
methods.description: Design reward structures so that self-interested agents collectively produce desired outcomes. Align individual incentives with system goals.

methods.name: Market Mechanism
methods.description: Use price signals and decentralized exchange to allocate scarce resources efficiently without central planning.

methods.name: Option Thinking
methods.description: Value and create flexibility: delay irreversible decisions, create the right (but not obligation) to act later when more information is available.

methods.name: Neuroevolution
methods.description: Combine evolutionary algorithms (population search) with neural networks (function approximation) to evolve both structure and weights.

methods.name: Simulated Annealing
methods.description: Start with high 'temperature' allowing exploration (accept worse solutions), then gradually cool to converge on optimum. Balance exploration vs exploitation.

methods.name: Mechanism Design + Feedback
methods.description: Design rules of the game (mechanism) so rational agents' equilibrium behavior produces desired outcomes, then add feedback loops to adjust rules dynamically.

methods.name: Molecular Computing
methods.description: Encode information and computation in molecular structures and reactions, achieving massive parallelism through chemistry rather than electronics.
